title: Array type function arguments should not decay to pointers
description: Array type function arguments should not decay to pointersAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode Smellcppcoreguidelinesbased-on-misrapitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhen a variable with array type decays to a pointer, its bounds are lost.If a design requires arrays of different lengths, then a class should be used to encapsulate the array objects and so ensure that the size is maintained. Note that in C++20, the classstd::spanis designed for this purpose, and you can find implementation ofspanthat works with earlier versions of the standard.Noncompliant code examplevoid f1( int p[ 10 ] ); // Non-compliant, function called with arguments of array type void f2( int *p ); // Non-compliant, function is called with arguments of array type void functionWorkingOnSingleI
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: void f1(int p[10]); // Non-compliant: array decays to pointer, bounds lost
void f2(int *p); // Non-compliant: pointer parameter loses array information
void processArray(int arr[]); // Non-compliant: array size information lost
compliant: void f1(std::span<int> p); // Compliant: using std::span preserves bounds
void f2(std::array<int, 10>& arr); // Compliant: using std::array preserves size

template<size_t N>
void processArray(std::array<int, N>& arr); // Compliant: template preserves size
void f3(int (&arr)[10]); // Compliant: reference to array preserves bounds

checks.message: Array type function arguments should not decay to pointers
